elements.sent-2411-001: Under the assumption that a finite signal with different sampling lengths or different sampling frequencies is considered as equivalent signals, the signal space is considered as the quotient space of $\R^{\infty}$ over equivalence.
elements.sent-2411-002: The topological structure and the properties of signal space are investigated.
elements.sent-2411-003: Using them some characteristics of semi-tensor product based compressed sensing (STP-CS) are revealed.
elements.sent-2411-004: Finally, a systematic analysis of the construction of sensing matrix based on balanced incomplete block design (BIBD) is presented.
elements.sent-2411-005: The compressed sensing (CS), as a new technique for signal processing, was initially proposed by Donoho, Candes, Tao et al. in 2006 \cite{don06,can06 .}
elements.sent-2411-006: The basic idea of CS is to compress a signal $x$ of dimension $n$ through a matrix $A\in {\cal M _{m\times n}$, $m<<n$, called the sensing matrix, to get a sampled data $y\in R^m$.. That is, \begin{equation}\label{1.1} y=Ax.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-007: The sensing matrix is obtained as follows: Assume the original signal is $\theta\in \R^n$, and the sampled data is $y=\Phi \theta\in \R^m$.
elements.sent-2411-008: Then CS tries to design $\Psi$ such that $\theta=\Psi x$.
elements.sent-2411-009: Then the sensing matrix is obtained as $A=\Phi \Psi$.
elements.sent-2411-010: Since $x$ is constructed in such a way, it is possible to recover $x$ from sampled date $y$ \cite{can06b,can07 .}
elements.sent-2411-011: A fundamental characteristic is the spark of $A$, which is the smallest number of the dependent columns of $A$.
elements.sent-2411-012: Then the follows is a criterion to justify if the original signal can be recovered from sampled data.
elements.sent-2411-013: Denote by $w(x)$ the number of nonzero entries in vector $x$, and $\Sigma^n_k$ the set of $x\in \R^n$ with $w(x)\leq k$.
elements.sent-2411-014: Then we have the following result.
elements.sent-2411-015: Assume $\spark(A)>2k$, then the equation has at most one solution $x\in \Sigma^n_k$.
elements.sent-2411-016: Construct the sensing matrix is one of the fundamental issues in CS.
elements.sent-2411-017: It has been investigated by many authors.
elements.sent-2411-018: For instance, \cite{do12,cle14 proposed a technique to construct a structurally random matrix for the sensing matrix.}
elements.sent-2411-019: Many deterministic sensing matrices have been constructed \cite{ami11,xu14,yua15 .}
elements.sent-2411-020: To reduce the storage space of the sensing matrices, many methods have been developed, including orthogonal bases \cite{dua12 , low-rank matrices \cite{lee13,cai14 , etc. Semi-tensor product (STP) of matrices/vectors is a generalization of conventional matrix/vector product, which removes the dimension restriction on factor matrices/vectors of the conventional one\cite{che12 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: The STP has firstly been used to CS by the pioneer work of \cite{xie16 , where the technique is named as STP-CS.}
elements.sent-2411-022: The basic idea for STP-CS is to replace fundamental CS-model (\ref{1.1 ) by \begin{equation}\label{1.2} y=A\ltimes x (=(A_0\otimes I_s)x),\end{equation} where $A_0\in {\cal M _{m_0\times n_0}$, $m=m_0s$ and $n=n_0s$.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: There are two obvious advantages of STP-CS: (i) It can reduce the storage of sensing matrix; (ii) it can be used for all $n$ dimensional signals as long as $m|n$.
elements.sent-2411-024: There are some fundamental characteristics which are used to evaluate the quality of sensing matrices.
elements.sent-2411-025: Unfortunately, in general, verifying RIP is difficult.
elements.sent-2411-026: An alternative criterion is the coherence.
elements.sent-2411-027: A sufficient condition for a lossless recovering the signal is \cite{dua11 \begin{equation}\label{1.6} k<\frac{1}{2}\left(1+\frac{1}{\mu(A)}\right).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-028: Hence the smaller the $\mu(A)$ the larger the $k$ is allowed, which means more signals can be treated.
elements.sent-2411-029: Since then, there have been considerable researches for STP-CS.
elements.sent-2411-030: For instance, a further development of STP-CS called the $PTP-CS$ is presented by \cite{pen19 , \cite{lia22 considered how to construct a random sensing matrix for STP-CS.}}
elements.sent-2411-031: Some applications shown the efficiency of STP-CS.
elements.sent-2411-032: For instance, the design of one-bit compressed sensing by STP-CS was proposed by \cite{hou23 , \cite{cha23 uses STP-CS for secure medical image transmission; the problem of reducing storage space of sensing matrix using STP-CS was investigated by \cite{wan17 ; a secure image encryption scheme by using STP-CS is proposed by \cite{wen20 ; \cite{jos19 considered the storage constrained smart meter sensing using STP-CS; \cite{ye23 proposed a reversible image hiding algorithm using STP-CS, etc. The purpose of this paper is threefold: (1) Propose the structure of signal space.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-033: The structure of signal space provides a solid framework for design of STP-CS.
elements.sent-2411-034: The rest of this paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-035: Section 2 is a systematic review on both left and right STPs, their quotient spaces, and their vector space structure.
elements.sent-2411-036: Section 3 investigates the topological structure of the signal space, which is the quotient space of cross-dimensional Euclidian space, and its topology is deduced from distance.
elements.sent-2411-037: In Section 4 the signal space is embedded into various functional spaces, including Fr\.{e chet space, Balach space, and Hilbert space.}
elements.sent-2411-038: These functional spaces provide different structures for signal space, which reveals related properties of signal space.
elements.sent-2411-039: Section 5 provides a generalized dimension free STP-CS, which contains the STP-CS in current literature as its spacial case.
elements.sent-2411-040: An precise lossless recovering condition for STP-CS is obtained.
elements.sent-2411-041: Section 6 proposes a new constructing technique for balanced incomplete block design (BIBD) based design of sensing matrix.
elements.sent-2411-042: Finally, Section 7 is a brief conclusion.
elements.sent-2411-043: Before ending this section we give a list of notations.
elements.sent-2411-044: The left matrix-matrix (MM-) STP is defined by \begin{equation}\label{2.1} A\ltimes B:=\left(A\otimes I_{t/n}\right)\left(B\otimes I_{t/p}\right)\in {\cal M}_{mt/n\times qt/p}.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-004: \begin{equation}\label{2.1} A\ltimes B:=\left(A\otimes I_{t/n}\right)\left(B\otimes I_{t/p}\right)\in {\cal M}_{mt/n\times qt/p}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-045: The right MM-STP is defined by \begin{equation}\label{2.2} A\rtimes B:=\left( I_{t/n}\otimes A\right)\left(I_{t/p}\otimes B\right)\in {\cal M}_{mt/n\times qt/p}.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-005: \begin{equation}\label{2.2} A\rtimes B:=\left( I_{t/n}\otimes A\right)\left(I_{t/p}\otimes B\right)\in {\cal M}_{mt/n\times qt/p}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-046: The left matrix-vector (MV-) STP is defined by \begin{equation}\label{2.3} A\lvtimes x:=\left(A\otimes I_{s/n}\right)\left(x\otimes \J_{s/r}\right)\in \R^s.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-047: The right MV-STP is defined by \begin{equation}\label{2.4} A\rvtimes x:=\left( I_{s/n}\otimes A\right)\left(\J_{s/r}\otimes x\right)\in \R^s.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-048: And the set of finite-dimensional vectors by \begin{equation}\R^{\infty}=\bigcup_{n=1}^{\infty}\R^n.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-049: The basic properties of STPs are listed as follows: \begin{prp}\cite{che12 Let $A,B,C\in {\cal M}$, $x,y\in \R^{\infty}$.}
elements.sent-2411-050: When $n=p$, the MM-STPs are degenerated to conventional MM-product.. That is, \begin{equation}\label{2.5} A\ltimes B=A\rtimes B=AB.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-051: When $n=r$, the MV-STPs are degenerated to conventional MV-product.. That is, \begin{equation}\label{2.6} A\lvtimes x=A\rvtimes x=Ax.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-052: Two matrices $A$ and $B$ are said to be left equivalent, denoted by $A\sim_{\ell B$, if there exist two identities $I_{\a}$ and $I_{\b}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.15} A\otimes I_{\a}=B\otimes I_{\b}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-053: The left equivalence class of $A$ is denoted by \begin{equation}\langle A\rangle_{\ell}:=\{B\;|\; B\sim_{\ell} A\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-054: Two matrices $A$ and $B$ are said to be right equivalent, denoted by $A\sim_r B$, it there exists two identities $I_{\a}$ and $I_{\b}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.16} I_{\a}\otimes A=I_{\b}\otimes B.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-055: The right equivalence class of $A$ is denoted by \begin{equation}\langle A\rangle_r:=\{B\;|\; B\sim_r A\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-056: Two vectors $x$ and $y$ are said to be left equivalent, denoted by $x\lra_{\ell y$, if there exist two 1-vectors $\J_{\a}$ and $\J_{\b}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.17} x\otimes \J_{\a}=y\otimes \J_{\b}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-057: The left equivalence class of $x$ is denoted by \begin{equation}\bar{x}_{\ell}:=\{y\;|\; y\lra_{\ell} x\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-058: Two vectors $x$ and $y$ are said to be right equivalent, denoted by $x\lra_r y$, if there exist two 1-vectors $\J_{\a}$ and $\J_{\b}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.18} \J_{\a}\otimes x=\J_{\b}\otimes y.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-059: The right equivalence class of $x$ is denoted by \begin{equation}\bar{x}_r:=\{y\;|\; y\lra_r x\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-060: If $A\sim_{\ell B$, then there exists a $C\in {\cal M}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.19} A=C\otimes I_{b},\quad B=C\otimes I_{a}.\end{equation}. w.l.g.}
elements.sent-2411-061: If $A\sim_r B$, then there exists a $C\in {\cal M}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.20} A= I_{b}\otimes C,\quad B= I_{a}\otimes C.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-020: \begin{equation}\label{2.20} A= I_{b}\otimes C,\quad B= I_{a}\otimes C.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-063: If $x\lra_{\ell y$, then there exists a $z\in \R^{\infty}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.21} x=z\otimes \J_{b},\quad y=z\otimes \J_{a}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-065: If $x\lra_r y$, then there exists a $z\in \R^{\infty}$ such that \begin{equation}\label{2.22} x=\J_{b}\otimes z,\quad y=\J_{a}\otimes z.\end{equation}.

(correspondingly,~\langle A\rangle_r=\{A_n=I_n\otimes A^r_0\;|\; n\in \Z^+\}.) \end{equation}.

(correspondingly,~\bar{x}_r=\{x_n=\J_n\otimes x^r_0\;|\; n\in \Z^+\}.) \end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-073: The left equivalent classes form the left quotient space as \begin{equation}\label{2.27} \Sigma_{\ell}:={\cal M}/\sim_{\ell}=\{\A_{\ell}\;|\; A\in {\cal M}\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-074: The right equivalent classes form the right quotient space as \begin{equation}\label{2.28} \Sigma_r:={\cal M}/\sim_r=\{\A_r\;|\; A\in {\cal M}\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-075: The left equivalent classes form the left quotient space as \begin{equation}\label{2.29} \Omega_{\ell}:=\R^{\infty}/\lra_{\ell}=\{\bar{x}_{\ell}\;|\; x\in \R^{\infty}\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-076: The right equivalent classes form the right quotient space as \begin{equation}\label{2.30} \Omega_r:=\R^{\infty}/\lra_r=\{\bar{x}_r\;|\; x\in \R^{\infty}\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-077: It is easy to see that the left (STP) system, including the left STP, left equivalence, and left quotient space, is ``mirror symmetric" to the right (STP) system, including the right STP, right equivalence, and right quotient space.
elements.sent-2411-078: Up to this paper, all the researches are concentrated mainly on the left system.
elements.sent-2411-079: In the rest of this paper, we are only interested on left system too.
elements.sent-2411-080: We claim that all the results obtained for the left system are also available for the right system.

elements.sent-2411-357: The purpose of this paper is to reveal some mathematical foundations for the STP-CS.
elements.sent-2411-358: First, the signal space is presented.
elements.sent-2411-359: The STP based equivalence leads to a quotient space, which is the signal space.
elements.sent-2411-360: Second, a coordinate-free STP-CS is presented.
elements.sent-2411-361: It is also revealed that STP-CS has an advantage in $\ell_0$.
elements.sent-2411-362: Third, a systematic construction of BIBD-based sensing matrix is obtained.
